title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 25)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Recent home sales in Abington include a three-family flat for $875,000 and a one-family ranch for $750,000.
A one-family Cape Cod in Acton sold for $1,325,000, featuring 9 rooms and 5 baths on a 24,000-square-foot lot.
In Boston, a condo on Commonwealth Ave. sold for $6,725,000, while another on Marlborough St. sold for $4,500,000.

title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, convicted of aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery for 1989 Framingham store assaults.
Gale was captured in 2024 after evading police for over 30 years, identified through modern DNA technology.
Gale led police on an 82-minute car chase in Los Angeles before being arrested and extradited to Massachusetts.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence's head coach, succeeding at South Florida with a 25-9 record.
Hodgson made a viral joke about his recruiting abilities, referencing his wife, Jordan, during his introduction.
Providence aims to rebuild under Hodgson after two sub-.500 seasons, emphasizing his recruiting and coaching skills.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children in foster care.
Castrichini was inspired by attending the Boston CASA Gala, where he learned about the challenges foster children face.
As a board member of Boston CASA, he aims to raise awareness and encourage support for foster kids.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston teachers, parents, and students protested proposed cuts of over 400 jobs, primarily affecting high-needs students.
The Boston Teachers Union requested a $48 million investment to avoid layoffs affecting multilingual and special education students.
Boston City Councilors debated school budget cuts as student enrollment decreases, with three schools set to close by 2027.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests scheduled in Massachusetts on March 28, including a major rally at Boston Common.
Massachusetts ranks second to California in number of protests, part of over 3,000 events planned nationwide.
Protests oppose policies like Trump's immigration crackdown and reflect concerns over democracy and U.S. actions in Iran.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is a leading candidate for NBA MVP, with support from ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts Wilbon and Kornheiser.
Brown averages career highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.1 assists, leading Celtics to second place in the Eastern Conference.
Celtics legend Paul Pierce argues Brown deserves more recognition for maintaining the team's competitive edge amid roster changes and injuries.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police are searching for Callahan Pierce, an 8-year-old boy last seen Tuesday night near Moraine Street.
Callahan Pierce was last seen wearing a black sweatshirt and sweatpants and carrying a backpack around 236 Moraine St.
Residents are urged to check home cameras for sightings and contact police at 781-834-6655 with any information.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement and will debut with the Providence Bruins on Wednesday.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney stated there's no rush to move Hagens to the NHL during Boston's playoff push.
Hagens' potential call-up to the NHL depends on his adaptation to the professional level in upcoming games.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, was sentenced to life without parole for the 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin.
Tracy Gilpin was reported missing on October 2, 1986, and her body was found 20 days later in Myles Standish State Park.
Charges of kidnapping and assault to rape were dropped due to expired statute of limitations; the murder case lay dormant for 32 years.
